story.iWant: main `prisma/seed.ts` entry point and organized seeder structure
story.soThat: `npx prisma db seed` komutu çalıştığında tüm seeders sırayla çalışsın
story.tasks: 5 tasks with 25 subtasks covering entry point creation, seeder files implementation, individual seeder classes, error handling, and testing

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. prisma/seed.ts main entry point, 2. prisma/seeders/ directory, 3. Individual seeder files (user, role, permission, file), 4. Main seed function with PrismaClient, 5. Seeder execution order (roles → permissions → users), 6. Progress logging with ✓ checkmarks, 7. Error handling with try-catch, 8. Prisma disconnect in finally block

doc.title: Epic 13: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.section: Story 13.1: Prisma Native Seeding Entry Point
doc.snippet: Main entry point at prisma/seed.ts implementing Prisma's native seeding feature. Modular seeder pattern with dependency-aware execution order.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Seeder Structure: prisma/seed.ts entry point, prisma/seeders/ individual seeder classes with static seed() methods.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Seeding Order: 1. RoleSeeder → 2. PermissionSeeder → 3. UserSeeder → 4. FileSeeder → 5. SmsSeeder

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Transaction Support: All-or-nothing seeding. Error Handling: try-catch with process.exit(1) on failure. Prisma Disconnect: $disconnect() in finally block.

doc.title: Architecture - Boilerplate
doc.snippet: Test Coverage Requirements: Unit tests, Integration tests, E2E tests with proper test utilities.

doc.title: Epic 12: Code Quality & Standards
doc.snippet: eslint-plugin-import configured with hrsync-backend 8-category pattern for consistent import organization across codebase.

code.reason: Demonstrates 8-category import pattern that seeders should follow
Type assertion patterns for Prisma and external libraries
CI/CD integration patterns for database operations
Modular architecture pattern similar to seeder organization

story-context.constraints: Required Patterns: Modular seeder classes with static seed() methods, Dependency injection not required (PrismaClient as parameter), No NestJS module dependencies. Layer Restrictions: Standalone TypeScript classes only, No controllers/DTOs needed, Database operations only. Testing Requirements: Unit tests for each seeder class, Integration tests for end-to-end seeding, Error handling validation. Coding Standards: Follow Epic 12 import organization (8-category), TypeScript strict mode enabled, Progress logging with ✓/❌ emojis.

interface.name: Seeder Interface
interface.signature: class SeederName { static async seed(prisma: PrismaClient): Promise<void> }

tests.standards: Jest v29+ for unit/integration testing. Seeder tests require beforeEach/afterEach cleanup. End-to-end tests validate full seed execution with transaction rollback on failure. TypeScript strict mode applies to all test files. Import organization follows 8-category pattern from Epic 12.
tests.locations: test/seeders/ for individual seeder unit tests, test/integration/ for end-to-end seeding tests, test/ for global test setup/teardown

ideas.test: Verify async main() executes all seeders in correct order and handles errors properly
Verify roles → permissions → users → files → SMS dependency chain
Verify upsert() and createMany(skipDuplicates: true) prevent duplicates
Verify console.log outputs with ✓ checkmarks appear during seeding
Verify try-catch block logs errors with ❌ and exits with process.exit(1)
